claim: Abandoned galgos living rough require weeks to months of patient observation and food-coaxing before allowing any human approach.
stance_target: galgos in lost/feral state show extreme approach avoidance requiring prolonged habituation
scope_conditions: Galgos abandoned in Spain, rescued by Galgos del Sol; feral or semi-feral state after abandonment
quoted_support: An abandoned galgo that has been living rough may need to be patiently observed and coaxed with food for weeks—or sometimes months—before allowing a human to come near.

claim: Abandoned galgos are found in extreme locations — wells, traffic, fields, roadsides — confirming a wide-ranging flight pattern rather than staying in one place.
stance_target: galgos when lost/abandoned do not shelter in place but move into dangerous terrain
scope_conditions: Galgos del Sol rescue operations in Spain
quoted_support: GDS has rescued galgos found in wells, lying injured in fields, abandoned in dumpsters, running terrified through traffic, giving birth on roadsides, or left to die in deserted breeding camps.

claim: Galgos are athletic jumpers capable of clearing 4-foot barriers, making standard containment measures insufficient when searching for a lost galgo's refuge.
stance_target: galgo containment/search radius modeling must account for high vertical mobility
quoted_support: they are curious and surprisingly athletic jumpers, meaning baby gates and low fences (such as four-foot barriers) are often not sufficient to contain them.

claim: Galgo rescue operations require round-the-clock availability because sightings of lost/abandoned dogs occur unpredictably at any hour.
stance_target: galgo rescue logistics: time-of-day unpredictability requires 24/7 alert protocols
quoted_support: Because sightings can occur at any time, the GDS rescue team remains on call around the clock.

claim: Some galgos exhibit specific fear responses including fear of men and fear of passing through open doors when a person is nearby, consistent with trauma-conditioned avoidance.
stance_target: galgo trauma baseline produces specific approach avoidance triggers beyond generalized fear
quoted_support: Some may exhibit a fear of men. Others may be scared to go through an open door while you are standing next to it.
